solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Copilot Pages branching and mutability states are not documented lifecycle events
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "record branching and mutability lineage events"; "Branching event log"; "Page branching, fork, and mutability transition events"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn documents Copilot Pages/Notebooks governance around SharePoint Embedded storage, audit logs, retention, and version history. It does not document branch/fork/merge or mutable/locked/preserved Page states as Microsoft product lifecycle events, so the current wording can lead implementers to build evidence around unsupported signals.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace branch/fork/merge/mutability wording with documented signals such as SharePoint Embedded .page files, Purview audit events, retention labels/policies, and version history. If branch-like lineage remains, label it as an internal sample taxonomy rather than a Microsoft Pages state.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Version History: Export in Purview or via Graph API... 50 versions per file (not configurable).

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: OneNote retention is section-based, not notebook-level label assignment
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "OneNote Notebook retention-policy coverage and inheritance"; "OneNote Notebook retention-label assignment"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The README implies that a OneNote notebook itself receives a retention label or policy assignment. Microsoft Learn says retention settings apply to OneNote sections as files; pages inherit from their parent section, and the notebook modified date is not used by Microsoft 365 retention.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Describe OneNote evidence as section/folder-level retention coverage within the parent SharePoint or OneDrive location, with notebook identity used only as grouping metadata.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Only sections are impacted by the retention settings that you specify.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Loop API wording does not match current Microsoft Learn surfaces
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "wiring live Microsoft Graph, Loop, and Purview data sources"; "Microsoft Graph and Loop API insertion points"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Current Microsoft Learn pages describe Loop and Copilot Pages/Notebooks content through SharePoint Embedded, SharePoint/OneDrive, Purview, and Graph export/drive surfaces. They do not document a standalone Microsoft Loop API for this inventory pattern.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace "Loop API" with "SharePoint Embedded containers, Microsoft Graph DriveItem/export where documented, Purview audit/retention, and Loop/Cloud Policy admin surfaces."
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Loop content is stored in SharePoint, OneDrive, and SharePoint Embedded.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Branching audit terminology should be qualified as internal sample taxonomy
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "branching audit"; "sample-data inventory of Pages, Notebooks, Loop components, and branching events"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The changelog presents branching audit as part of the Microsoft technical scaffold, but Microsoft Learn documents version history and audit logs for Copilot Pages/Notebooks rather than Page branch/fork/merge lifecycle events.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Clarify that branching events are representative internal sample lineage, or rename the capability to documented version-history/audit-log evidence.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Version History: Export in Purview or via Graph API... 50 versions per file (not configurable).

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Checklist uses unsupported branching and vague Loop integration surfaces
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "summary branching audit"; "full branching audit"; "Live Microsoft Graph, Loop, and Purview integration insertion points"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn documents Purview audit logs, version history, SharePoint Embedded containers, and Graph export surfaces for Copilot Pages/Notebooks. It does not document Page branching audit events or a standalone Loop API integration surface.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Update the checklist to validate documented version-history/audit-log evidence and name current integration surfaces: SharePoint Embedded, Microsoft Graph DriveItem/export where documented, Purview audit/retention, and Cloud Policy/SharePoint admin controls.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Audit logs: Available for all events. Retained, exportable, and streamable to third-party tools.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Graph beta Pages enumeration and Microsoft Loop API claims are unsupported
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "Microsoft Graph (Copilot Pages, OneNote, Loop) - live target"; "Microsoft Loop APIs can expose Pages..."; "Microsoft Graph beta endpoints for Copilot Pages enumeration"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The architecture identifies non-documented integration surfaces. Current Microsoft Learn content describes Copilot Pages/Notebooks storage in SharePoint Embedded and supports Graph API export for third-party tools; it does not document a Microsoft Graph beta enumeration endpoint for Copilot Pages or a standalone Microsoft Loop API for this purpose.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Rewrite the integration model around documented surfaces: SharePoint Embedded containers and container URLs, Microsoft Graph DriveItem/export capabilities where documented, Purview audit/retention, and Cloud Policy/SharePoint admin controls. Do not reference Graph beta Pages enumeration unless Microsoft publishes a specific endpoint.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Graph API export: Supported for third-party tools.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Branch/fork/mutability lifecycle is not documented for Copilot Pages
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "mutability and branching lineage"; "Page branching, fork, and mutability transitions"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn documents Copilot Pages/Notebooks audit logs and version history, but not branch, fork, merge, or mutability-state events as product lifecycle signals.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace the Branching Event Recorder with a version-history/audit-log evidence component, or explicitly mark branch/fork/mutability as repository sample taxonomy not backed by Microsoft product events.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Version History: Export in Purview or via Graph API... 50 versions per file (not configurable).

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: OneNote retention resolver is modeled at the wrong granularity
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "retention label assigned to each Notebook"; "OneNote Notebook retention-label assignment"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft 365 retention for OneNote content applies to sections as files, with pages inheriting from their parent section. Modeling direct notebook-level retention labels or notebook policy inheritance is inaccurate.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Model OneNote sections/folders and parent SharePoint/OneDrive retention coverage; keep notebook metadata only for grouping and reporting.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Pages from each section are contained within the file and inherit the retention settings from their parent section.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Copilot Pages retention labels need limited-application caveats
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "records the retention-label assignment... for each Page"; "Resolve retention labels and policies that apply to Copilot Pages"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The architecture omits important Microsoft Learn caveats: retention labels for Copilot Pages have limited manual application; they cannot be viewed or applied directly in Copilot Pages, and record/regulatory record labels cannot be manually applied in Copilot Pages or Loop.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Add a caveat that Copilot Pages label evidence should be resolved via OneDrive/SharePoint/Loop/Purview-supported paths and distinguish retention policies from limited retention-label support.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Retention labels cannot be viewed or applied directly from a Copilot Page.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Deployment guide treats branching audit as a Microsoft Pages signal
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "summary branching audit"; "full branching audit"; "Pages inventory with retention labels and mutability state"; "Branching event records"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn documents Copilot Pages/Notebooks audit logs and version history, not branch/fork/merge audit modes or mutability states.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace branching audit validation with documented Purview audit-log, version-history, retention-policy, and retention-label checks. If branch events remain, mark them as sample-only internal lineage.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Audit logs: Available for all events. Retained, exportable, and streamable to third-party tools.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Notebook-retention-log schema should track OneNote sections/folders
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "OneNote Notebook retention-policy assignment and inheritance lineage" with `notebookId`, `retentionLabel`, and `retentionPolicySource`
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn states OneNote retention impacts sections as files and pages inherit from sections. The current schema implies direct notebook-level assignment and inheritance.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Revise the schema to include section-level identifiers and parent notebook/container metadata, or explicitly state that notebook-level rows summarize section/folder retention coverage.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Only sections are impacted by the retention settings that you specify.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Branching-event-log schema uses undocumented Page lifecycle events
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: `mutabilityState`; `branchingParentPageId`; `eventType`: `branch`, `fork`, `merge`, `lock`, or `preserve`
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn documents Copilot Pages/Notebooks version history and audit logs, not branch/fork/merge event types or mutable/locked/preserved Page states.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace these fields with documented version-history and Purview audit fields, or label them as sample-only internal lineage states that are not emitted by Microsoft 365.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Version History: Export in Purview or via Graph API... 50 versions per file (not configurable).

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Copilot Pages retention-label evidence needs limited-support caveat
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "retentionLabel: Microsoft Purview retention label assigned to the Page"; "Records retention-label coverage... for Copilot Pages"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn says retention labels are supported for Copilot Pages with limited manual application and cannot be viewed or applied directly from a Copilot Page. Record/regulatory record labels also cannot be manually applied from Copilot Pages or Loop.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Add these caveats and distinguish policy coverage from label assignment evidence.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Retention labels cannot be viewed or applied directly from a Copilot Page.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Microsoft Copilot prerequisite should use Microsoft 365 Copilot licensing
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "Microsoft 365 E3 or E5 tenant with Microsoft Copilot..."
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn names the product/license as Microsoft 365 Copilot and describes it as an add-on plan with prerequisites. The current "Microsoft Copilot" wording is ambiguous and can be confused with consumer Copilot.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: State that in-scope users need Microsoft 365 Copilot licensing with a qualifying Microsoft 365 E3/E5 (or equivalent) subscription, plus enabled OneNote, Loop, SharePoint Online, and Copilot Pages/Notebooks where applicable.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Microsoft 365 Copilot is an add-on plan with specific licensing prerequisites.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Loop and Copilot Pages read scopes are not documented prerequisites
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: `Notes.Read.All`; `Sites.Read.All`; "Loop and Copilot Pages read scopes as released by Microsoft for the tenant"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The OneNote `Notes.Read.All` permission is documented for notebook listing, but Microsoft Learn does not document generic Loop or Copilot Pages read scopes for this inventory. Current Copilot Pages/Notebooks management guidance uses SharePoint Embedded containers, Purview, and Graph export/DriveItem capabilities where documented.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Keep `Notes.Read.All` for OneNote. Replace the Loop/Copilot Pages read-scope bullet with documented prerequisites such as SharePoint Embedded administrator access for container discovery, SharePoint/Graph file permissions where applicable, and Purview permissions for audit/retention lookup.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Sign in to the SharePoint admin center with the SharePoint Embedded administrator role.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Purview Compliance Admin is not the documented role name
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "Purview Compliance Admin for retention-policy lookup"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn recommends the Compliance Administrator admin role group for retention policies/labels, or a custom role group with Retention Management/View-Only Retention Management. It also uses Records Management for records-management tasks. "Purview Compliance Admin" is not the documented role name.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Use "Compliance Administrator" or a custom role group with "Retention Management"/"View-Only Retention Management" for retention-policy lookup; use "Records Management" for records/disposition tasks.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: we recommend that you add users to the Compliance Administrator admin role group.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Copilot Pages storage location is SharePoint Embedded, not SharePoint or OneDrive containers
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "retention policies pre-published for the SharePoint and OneDrive containers that host Copilot Pages and OneNote Notebooks"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn states Copilot Pages and Copilot Notebooks are stored in a user-owned SharePoint Embedded container. OneNote content can be in SharePoint/OneDrive, but Copilot Pages should be documented separately from OneNote locations.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Reference SharePoint Embedded containers for Copilot Pages, and SharePoint/OneDrive folders or sections for OneNote content. For Purview retention, mention All SharePoint Sites or specific SharePoint Embedded container URLs where applicable.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Copilot Pages and Copilot Notebooks are stored within your organization in SharePoint Embedded.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Copilot Pages enumeration troubleshooting assumes an undocumented Graph surface
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "Confirm Copilot Pages enumeration is enabled in the tenant..."; "until live Graph access is wired"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn does not document a Microsoft Graph Copilot Pages enumeration endpoint. Current guidance for Copilot Pages/Notebooks centers on SharePoint Embedded containers, Purview audit/export, and Graph export/DriveItem capabilities where documented.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace this with checks for SharePoint Embedded container discovery, Purview audit/export visibility, and documented Graph DriveItem/export access. Avoid instructing users to enable a non-documented Pages enumeration feature.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Graph API export: Supported for third-party tools.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: OneNote retention troubleshooting should focus on sections and folders
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "Notebook shows `none` for retention-policy source"; "parent SharePoint site or OneDrive library for an applied retention label"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn says retention settings impact OneNote sections as files and pages inherit from those sections. The troubleshooting guidance should not imply direct notebook-level retention assignment.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Troubleshoot OneNote section/folder retention, parent SharePoint/OneDrive policy scope, and retention labels applied to OneNote folders or sections.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Pages from each section are contained within the file and inherit the retention settings from their parent section.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Loop API reachability guidance references a non-documented API
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "Verify the Loop API is reachable from the deployment host"; "Microsoft Graph, Loop, or Purview endpoints"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn describes Loop content storage and administration through SharePoint Embedded, SharePoint/OneDrive, Cloud Policy, SharePoint PowerShell, Purview, and documented Graph export/file surfaces. A standalone Loop API is not documented for this scenario.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Replace Loop API reachability checks with checks for SharePoint Embedded container access, Graph DriveItem/export access where used, Purview audit/retention access, and Loop/Cloud Policy configuration.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Loop content is stored in SharePoint, OneDrive, and SharePoint Embedded.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Deployment manifest exposes unsupported branching audit as product evidence
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "branching audit mode"; `branchingEvent`; "branching audit [{3}]"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The script output presents branching audit as a Copilot Pages governance signal. Microsoft Learn documents audit logs and version history for Copilot Pages/Notebooks rather than Page branch/fork/merge lifecycle events.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Rename these manifest fields to documented audit/version-history evidence or label them as internal sample lineage not emitted by Microsoft 365.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Audit logs: Available for all events. Retained, exportable, and streamable to third-party tools.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Branching event export and Graph activity API note do not match documented Pages evidence
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "branching-event-log"; "live audit ingestion requires Microsoft Graph activity APIs"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Microsoft Learn documents Purview audit-log search/export and version history for Copilot Pages/Notebooks; it does not document branch/fork/merge event exports or a generic Microsoft Graph activity API for Pages lifecycle events.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Use Purview audit-log evidence fields and documented SharePoint Embedded/Graph file export/version-history signals; avoid implying a Graph activity API for unsupported branching events.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Search audit logs for "page" or "loop" or the Loop Application IDs.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Live source comments reference non-documented Loop and Pages APIs
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: "from Microsoft Graph, Microsoft Loop, and Microsoft Purview"; "live Graph or Loop implementation"
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: Current Microsoft Learn documentation does not describe a standalone Microsoft Loop API or a Graph Copilot Pages enumeration API for this inventory. The documented surfaces are SharePoint Embedded, Purview, Cloud Policy/SharePoint admin controls, and Graph export/file capabilities where applicable.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Revise comments and warning text to mention SharePoint Embedded/Purview/Graph DriveItem or export surfaces instead of "Microsoft Loop" reads or generic Graph/Loop implementation.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Copilot Pages and Copilot Notebooks are stored within your organization in SharePoint Embedded.

solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.title: Sample Page states and branching events are not Microsoft lifecycle states
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.current: `editable`, `branched`, `locked`, `preserved`; event types `branch`, `fork`, `merge`, `lock`, `preserve`
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.issue: The sample data uses Page states and branching event types that Microsoft Learn does not document for Copilot Pages/Notebooks. This can make sample evidence look like it came from real Microsoft product event names.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.suggestion: Rename the sample values to clearly internal taxonomy or replace them with documented Purview audit/version-history events.
solutions/22-pages-notebooks-retention-t.quote: Version History: Export in Purview or via Graph API... 50 versions per file (not configurable).
